replication_study.hypothesis: Participants in the attention condition (partner monitoring signal active 80% of trials) will report higher intimacy (sum of six items: a1, a2, a3, reverse-coded a4, a5, a6) than participants in the no attention condition (0% monitoring).

data_plan.source_type: Laboratory-style experiment dataset (computerized joint trivia task; survey responses for intimacy items)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single experiment sample; binary comparison: attention (1) vs no attention (0).
data_plan.access_details: Provided in replication package at data/original/37/input/replication_data. For execution, place or mount the file at /app/data/ohtsubo data.csv.

qualification.explanation: The dataset mirrors the original Study 2 paradigm: 20-item trivia task (q1–q20), binary attention manipulation (condition 0/1), and post-task intimacy ratings. It is an independent sample collected under similar procedures, enabling a robust test of the focal claim.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Original Study 2 measured intimacy after a 20-question trivia task with attention manipulated at 0% vs 80%, using a 7-point intimacy scale (four items in Study 2; original_paper.pdf, Sections 3.1.3 and 3.2.2). The replication dataset contains q1–q20 (suggesting 20 trials), a1–a6 for intimacy items, and a binary 'condition' variable (replication_data/ohtsubo data.csv; analysis ohtsubo.sps confirms use of condition 1 vs 0 and computation of intimacy total atot).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original Study 2 used four intimacy items (Cronbach’s alpha .72; original_paper.pdf, Section 3.1.3). The replication dataset sums six items (a1–a6) with a reverse-coded a4 (analysis ohtsubo.sps). Therefore, the intimacy construct is measured with 6 items instead of 4. Additionally, the original Study 2a sample was N=29 (original_paper.pdf, Section 3.2.1), while the replication dataset has N=41. These deviations are documented and retained for fidelity to the provided replication data.

data_plan.notes: Variables present: q1–q20 (task items), a1–a6 (intimacy items), age, sex, race, condition, a4r, atot. SPSS syntax shows reverse-coding of a4 (a4r = 8 - a4) and atot = a1+a2+a3+a4r+a5+a6, followed by t-test by condition (analysis ohtsubo.sps). Measurement differs from original (6 vs 4 items). All IO for the replication code is standardized to /app/data. Ensure the CSV is named exactly 'ohtsubo data.csv' and is accessible at /app/data.

steps: Run analysis_ohtsubo__py.py (entrypoint) to: (a) read /app/data/ohtsubo data.csv, (b) compute a4r = 8 - a4, (c) compute atot = a1+a2+a3+a4r+a5+a6, and mean-scale aint_mean = atot/6, (d) perform independent-samples t-tests (Welch and pooled) comparing atot by condition (1=attention, 0=no attention), (e) compute Cohen’s d and Hedges’ g, (f) compute Cronbach’s alpha for the six items.
Compare replication statistics (mean difference, t, p, effect size) with the original Study 2a benchmark (t(27)=5.91, p<.001, d=2.20).
planned_method.models: Independent-samples t-test (Welch’s unequal variances and pooled-variance variants) on atot; descriptive statistics; effect sizes (Cohen’s d, Hedges’ g); scale reliability (Cronbach’s alpha).
planned_method.outcome_variable: Intimacy total score atot (sum of a1, a2, a3, reverse-coded a4, a5, a6); mean-scale aint_mean = atot/6 for interpretability.
planned_method.control_variables: None (random assignment; sex examined but not included, consistent with original analyses in Study 2; original_paper.pdf Sections 3.2.2–3.4.2).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Difference in mean intimacy (atot) between attention and no attention groups; effect size (Cohen’s d, Hedges’ g).

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion for rows missing any of atot, aint_mean, or condition.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Single primary outcome; no multiple-testing correction required.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Two-sided alpha = 0.05; replication success emphasized by same direction (higher intimacy in attention), statistical significance (p<0.05), and comparable effect size magnitude relative to original (d≈2.20).

files.analysis_ohtsubo__py.py: Python translation of the SPSS analysis (analysis ohtsubo.sps). Reads /app/data/ohtsubo data.csv; reverse-codes a4 to a4r; computes atot and aint_mean; runs independent-samples t-tests (Welch and pooled) comparing attention (condition=1) vs no attention (condition=0); computes effect sizes and Cronbach’s alpha; writes results to /app/data/ohtsubo_ttest_results.json and a human-readable summary to /app/data/ohtsubo_ttest_summary.txt.

codebase.notes: All file IO is constrained to /app/data. Ensure the replication dataset is mounted at /app/data/ohtsubo data.csv before running the script. The script depends on pandas, numpy, and scipy.

instructions: Build or pull the Docker image with Python 3.10 and required packages.
Mount the local data directory to /app/data (e.g., -v ./data:/app/data).
analysis.comparison_metrics: Direction of effect (attention > no attention), t-statistic and p-value for atot, and effect size (Cohen’s d, Hedges’ g) benchmarked against the original Study 2a (t(27)=5.91, p<.001, d=2.20).
